key_hedging_phrases: our actual results could differ materially from those stated or implied in forward-looking statements
there can be no guarantee that contracts are not terminated or that contract options will be exercised
subject to termination provisions, including for convenience
many of which involve factors or circumstances that are beyond our control
RDV may exclude all or some portion of the value of certain commercial contracts
key_bullish_phrases: Palantir's Rule of 40 score is now an incredible 127%
We are an n of 1, and these numbers prove it
U.S. commercial revenue grew 137% year-over-year and 28% quarter-over-quarter to $507 million
Closed a record-setting $4.262 billion of total contract value, up 138% year-over-year
U.S. commercial revenue in excess of $3.144 billion, representing a growth rate of at least 115%
key_risk_phrases: our long and unpredictable sales cycle

the impact of recent, ongoing, or future global macroeconomic and geopolitical events, fluctuating interest rates, monetary policy changes, foreign currency fluctuations, or the potential or actual imposition of tariffs
issues raised by the use of artificial intelligence in our platforms
the fluctuation of our results of operations and our key business measures on a quarterly basis in future periods
news or social media coverage or other external scrutiny about us or our leadership
payload.management_tone_summary: Management tone is exceptionally bullish and self-assured, with CEO Karp using superlatives ('incredible,' 'record-setting,' 'n of 1') and explicitly framing the results as crushing consensus expectations — a unusually confident and assertive posture even relative to the already elevated tone of last quarter's Q3 2025 release. The incremental escalation from Q3 (Rule of 40 of 114%, U.S. commercial growth 121% YoY) to Q4 (Rule of 40 of 127%, U.S. commercial growth 137% YoY) is matched by an even more aggressive FY 2026 guide, signaling management's conviction that the growth trajectory is accelerating rather than decelerating. The hedging language is almost entirely boilerplate legal safe harbor text, not reflective of genuine operational uncertainty communicated by management.
payload.analyst_revision_rationale: Sell-side analysts are likely underestimating the velocity of U.S. commercial revenue growth, which re-accelerated from 121% YoY in Q3 to 137% YoY in Q4 — a rare sequential acceleration at this scale — while the FY 2026 guide of 115%+ U.S. commercial growth implies a sustained run rate that most consensus models have not yet incorporated. The record-setting TCV of $4.262B (up 138% YoY) and U.S. commercial RDV of $4.38B (up 145% YoY) provide unusually strong leading-indicator visibility into forward revenue recognition, giving analysts strong grounds to revise revenue estimates upward for Q1 2026 and beyond. The Q1 2026 revenue guidance midpoint of $1.534B represents ~69% YoY growth, which if consensus was anchored to the prior FY 2025 growth rate of 56%, implies a material upward revision to both revenue and EPS estimates. Adjusted operating margin guidance of ~57% for Q1 2026 ($872M adjusted income from operations on $1.534B revenue) also implies significant upside to profitability estimates. Finally, the specificity and tightness of the guidance ranges (e.g., $1.532–$1.536B for Q1 revenue, a $4M range) signal management confidence and reduces the probability of a negative revision, further supporting a large upward revision signal.
